description: Upstream Node Runtime Replacement — replace the current polyfill/bootstrap Node implementation with an upstream-Node-shaped runtime. Start with pre-research on bootstrap-critical bindings, make upstream fs the first real module target, compile the backend through the existing WasmVM path, and then bring modules up one by one.

userStories.title: Build host-side pre-research probes for bootstrap-critical bindings
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need a small host-side probe harness so we can verify the riskiest upstream Node bindings before committing to the rebuild.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a focused probe harness under packages/nodejs/ or packages/secure-exec/tests/ that can execute upstream bootstrap assets without requiring the full replacement runtime first
Probe internal/bootstrap/realm and internal/bootstrap/node with the narrowest viable binding set
Add a module_wrap probe that compiles and executes a trivial ESM module or documents the exact blocker
Add a contextify probe that exercises node:vm createContext()/runInContext() or documents the exact blocker
Add uv and cares_wrap probes that exercise a minimal net listen/connect path and dns.lookup('localhost') or document the exact blocker
Write the results and any blockers into docs-internal/specs/upstream-node-runtime-plan.md or a linked preflight note
If any probe disproves the plan, record the exact blocker instead of guessing and stop short of broad implementation
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the explicit go/no-go story from the spec's pre-research gate. The purpose is to find out whether module_wrap, contextify, uv, and cares_wrap are bounded work or a design blocker.

userStories.title: Build standalone WasmVM backend probes for fs, sockets, DNS, and TTY
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need standalone WasmVM-targeted probes so we can verify that the backend operations we need fit the existing WebAssembly VM path.
acceptanceCriteria: Add standalone WasmVM-targeted probe programs for fs operations: open, read, write, stat, readdir, realpath
Add standalone WasmVM-targeted probe programs for socket operations: connect, listen, accept, read, write, close
Add standalone WasmVM-targeted probe programs for DNS operations and expected error paths
Add a standalone WasmVM-targeted probe for TTY/raw-mode behavior or document the exact missing kernel/libc piece
Compile the probes through the existing WasmVM path rather than through a custom one-off runtime
Add focused tests that run the probes through the sandbox and compare behavior to expected results
Document which backend operations fit cleanly into the existing WasmVM path and which need follow-up imports or ABI work
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: These probes only validate backend operations. They do not prove module_wrap, contextify, JS handle identity, or callback/event delivery for tcp_wrap/stream_wrap.

userStories.title: Pin current LTS and add upstream Node asset sync scaffolding
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need the replacement runtime pinned to the current Node LTS with deterministic asset sync tooling so we can rehost upstream assets without manual drift.
acceptanceCriteria: Record the chosen current LTS version and exact upstream commit in the new upstream asset metadata
Add a sync script such as scripts/sync-node-upstream-assets.ts that pulls assets from the pinned upstream fork checkout
Vendor the full pinned builtin JS tree required by the selected Node version into packages/nodejs/assets/upstream-node/
Add builtin-manifest and version metadata files that make the asset set reviewable and deterministic
Document expected fork/remotes and sync workflow in docs-internal/specs/upstream-node-runtime-plan.md
Focused tests or validation scripts pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Do not cherry-pick a few builtin files. The sync flow should vendor the complete pinned builtin tree that the selected Node version needs.

userStories.title: Scaffold the upstream runtime source tree and asset loader
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need the in-repo upstream runtime structure in place so later stories have a clean implementation surface.
acceptanceCriteria: Add the new upstream runtime directories under packages/nodejs/src/upstream/
Add an asset loader that can read the vendored upstream asset set and expose builtin sources/metadata to the runtime
Add initial runtime-driver/bootstrap-loader/internal-binding-registry scaffolding files with clear TODO boundaries
Keep the public NodeRuntime surface unified; use internal experimental entrypoints only where needed for probes and bring-up
Document the new layout in docs-internal/specs/upstream-node-runtime-plan.md if paths or responsibilities change
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This story is structure only. It should not try to implement the full loader yet.

userStories.title: Implement builtins registry, compileFunction, and setInternalLoaders
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need the Node builtins bootstrap contract implemented so upstream bootstrap code can construct internalBinding() and builtin require().
acceptanceCriteria: Implement an upstream builtin registry backed by the vendored manifest and source tree
Implement the builtins binding surface expected by internal/bootstrap/realm, including builtinIds, compileFunction, and setInternalLoaders
Compile builtin source with the correct Node-style wrapper signatures for the relevant bootstrap categories
Store the internal loaders produced by setInternalLoaders in runtime state for later builtin require() use
Add focused tests for builtin compilation, setInternalLoaders round-trip, and builtin cache behavior
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the first real bootstrap seam. If the current V8 runtime cannot support it from TypeScript alone, document and implement the minimum native/v8-runtime support instead of papering over it.

userStories.title: Boot internal/bootstrap/realm and internal/bootstrap/node in snapshot-free mode
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need Secure Exec to execute the upstream Node bootstrap path directly so we can prove the architecture before adding more module support.
acceptanceCriteria: Run internal/per_context/* and internal/bootstrap/realm successfully through the new upstream runtime path
Run internal/bootstrap/node successfully for a narrow entrypoint such as internal/main/eval_string
Keep the bring-up explicitly snapshot-free
Document the minimum bootstrap-critical binding set needed for the scoped bring-up
Add focused tests for bootstrap success in both standalone NodeRuntime and kernel-mounted runtime-driver mode
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Do not broaden scope here. Worker threads, REPL, watch mode, inspector, and test-runner entrypoints remain out of scope.

userStories.title: Implement the minimal host binding registry for bootstrap and fs first-light
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need a host-side internalBinding registry for the early blockers so upstream bootstrap and fs can load without the legacy polyfill loader.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement the minimal host-side registry for bootstrap-critical bindings including builtins, module_wrap, contextify, config, util, process_methods, uv, cares_wrap, buffer, constants, errors, timers, and modules as needed
Classify each binding as host-only, host-lifecycle-plus-backend, or deferred
Keep JS-visible wrapper identity, callback delivery, ref/unref state, and close semantics on the host side
Document any deferred bindings explicitly instead of leaving implicit gaps
Add focused tests proving the registry supports the scoped bootstrap plus fs-first path
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This story should not try to make every binding feature-complete. It should make the early blocker inventory explicit and working.

userStories.title: Make upstream fs the first real module target
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need upstream lib/fs.js to load and work through the new runtime so we can validate the architecture with a module that is large but still request/response-shaped.
acceptanceCriteria: require('fs') resolves through the new upstream runtime path rather than the old polyfill loader
Upstream fs sync APIs work for a narrow first-light subset: open/read/write/close/stat/readdir/realpath
Upstream callback-style fs APIs for the same subset work with correct callback delivery semantics
Add focused tests for upstream fs first-light in standalone and kernel-mounted modes
Document the exact fs v1 subset and the next deferred fs surfaces to implement
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: fs is the first real target after bootstrap. Keep the scope strict and explicit.

userStories.title: Compile a WasmVM-targeted backend artifact for fs bottom-half operations
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need the fs backend compiled through the normal WasmVM path so the replacement runtime can start using the WebAssembly VM instead of growing more host bridge code.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a Node backend artifact that compiles through the existing WasmVM/WASIX toolchain rather than a one-off runtime path
Expose a versioned ABI for the fs bottom-half operations needed by upstream fs first-light
Instantiate and call the backend internally from the Node runtime rather than exposing it as a user-facing command
Keep JS-visible object identity and callback delivery on the host side while moving only the fs bottom-half operations into the backend
Add focused tests proving the backend artifact is used for fs first-light operations
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The backend should be a normal WasmVM-targeted artifact invoked internally. It is a sidecar service module, not a CLI command.

userStories.title: Expand fs parity across promises, streams, validation, and conformance
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need fs behavior expanded beyond first-light so we can trust the replacement runtime before broadening into other modules.
acceptanceCriteria: Expand upstream fs coverage to include fs.promises for the implemented subset
Add upstream fs stream coverage for the supported subset where it is realistic at this phase
Match Node-style validation and error behavior for the implemented fs APIs
Run the relevant vendored node-conformance fs coverage and remove stale expectations for newly passing tests
Regenerate packages/secure-exec/tests/node-conformance/conformance-report.json and docs/nodejs-conformance-report.mdx when expectations change
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Do not jump to net/http before fs has a real confidence floor.

userStories.title: Replace the current polyfill/bootstrap Node path with the upstream runtime
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need the upstream runtime to become the canonical Node implementation so we stop maintaining the old path.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove the old polyfill-first bootstrap path from the active Node implementation once bootstrap plus fs first-light are green
Make the upstream bootstrap path the canonical Node runtime path
Delete or retire legacy-only loader/polyfill code that is no longer part of the replacement architecture
Update docs-internal/specs/upstream-node-runtime-plan.md and any nearby architecture notes if implementation details changed materially
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The goal is replacement, not permanent dual compatibility. Do not remove the old path before bootstrap plus fs first-light are green on the new one.

userStories.title: Bring up net on the replacement runtime
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need net to work on the replacement runtime so we can start exercising the host-lifecycle versus Wasm-backend split for stream and socket handles.
acceptanceCriteria: require('net') resolves through the replacement runtime path
Minimal net server/client behavior works with upstream JS and the new binding split
Host-owned handle identity, callback/event delivery, and ref/unref behavior remain on the host side
Socket bottom-half operations run through the WasmVM-targeted backend where implemented
Add focused tests for net listen/connect/read/write/close behavior
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is where tcp_wrap/stream_wrap assumptions get real. Do not push JS handle identity into Wasm.

userStories.title: Bring up http and https on the replacement runtime
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need upstream http/https to work on the replacement runtime so we can validate the network stack above net.
acceptanceCriteria: require('http') and require('https') resolve through the replacement runtime path
Upstream http server/client round-trips work through the replacement runtime
Add wire-level differential tests that compare raw HTTP bytes against host Node for at least one representative scenario
Run the relevant vendored node-conformance coverage for the implemented http/https subset and remove stale expectations for newly passing tests
Regenerate packages/secure-exec/tests/node-conformance/conformance-report.json and docs/nodejs-conformance-report.mdx when expectations change
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Do not hand-wave protocol correctness from same-code-path loopback tests alone.

userStories.title: Bring up readline and tty on the replacement runtime
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I need upstream readline and tty behavior on the replacement runtime so interactive PTY workflows can start using the new implementation.
acceptanceCriteria: require('readline') resolves through the replacement runtime path
Basic readline line input works in a PTY-backed test
TTY raw mode, terminal size, and signal-sensitive behavior work for the supported subset
Add focused kernel-mediated tests for PTY/raw-mode behavior against the replacement runtime
Focused tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Out-of-scope runtime features should still fail deterministically without breaking bootstrap.
